title: Rust FFI bindings for transformer ops
description:
Extend the Rust FFI layer to bridge the new Zig transformer ops into safe Rust APIs.

1. Extend `synapse/crates/synapse-sys/src/lib.rs` (or equivalent) with new `extern "C"` declarations matching the updated synapse.h:
   - syn_layernorm_forward
   - syn_scaled_dot_product_attention
   - syn_rope_forward
   - syn_causal_mask

2. Extend synapse-core with safe Rust wrappers:
   - `Tensor::layernorm(gamma, beta, eps) -> Result<Tensor>`
   - `Tensor::scaled_dot_product_attention(key, value, scale, causal) -> Result<Tensor>`
   - `Tensor::rope(cos_table, sin_table, offset) -> Result<Tensor>`
   - `Tensor::causal_mask(seq_len) -> Result<Tensor>`
   All wrap status codes into `Result<T, SynapseError>`.

Tests:
- FFI roundtrip: create Rust tensors, call each new FFI function through safe wrappers, verify results match expected output
- Error propagation: shape mismatches produce proper Rust errors
- Memory safety: no leaks in create/call/destroy cycle (test with 10K iterations)

Pass/fail:
- All new FFI functions correctly bridged
- No memory leaks in roundtrip cycles
- Zig errors correctly map to Result::Err
- All existing Rust tests still pass
